entries.title: War Ends In Public Catastrophe Not Hero Victory

context.label: War battle collapses into mass casualty aftermath

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Paranormal Liberation War ends with mass casualties, escaped villains, exposed secrets, devastated cities, and shattered public confidence.
content.injection: After the war, do not write society as relieved by a clean win. The dominant state is collapse, grief, and institutional damage.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

entries.title: Hero Casualties Change The Professional Landscape

context.label: War battle collapses into mass casualty aftermath

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The war removes, injures, or discredits many heroes, changing who can protect civilians and who the public trusts.
content.injection: Post-war scenes should account for depleted hero capacity and shaken legitimacy, not merely damaged buildings.

entries.title: Midnight Death Hits Students As A Teacher Loss

context.label: War battle collapses into mass casualty aftermath

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Midnight’s death matters to students not only as a casualty report but as the loss of a teacher who guided them during the Machia crisis.
content.injection: If Midnight’s death is referenced, connect it to student grief and the cost of putting children near war.

entries.title: Villain Prison Breaks Make The Country Unsafe

context.label: All For One triggers prison breaks

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: All For One’s prison break strategy releases many villains and turns post-war Japan into a broader security crisis.
content.injection: After Tartarus breaks, street-level safety should collapse. Pro heroes face too many threats and not enough trust or manpower.

entries.title: Public Trust In Heroes Breaks After Multiple Revelations

context.label: Public trust in hero society collapses

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Dabi’s broadcast, Hawks’ killing of Twice, Endeavor’s family history, mass casualties, and prison breaks combine to break public trust.
content.injection: In post-war society scenes, public anger should be broad and layered. It is not caused by one scandal alone.

entries.title: Civilians Become More Suspicious And Self-Protective

context.label: Public trust in hero society collapses

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After the war, many civilians lose faith in professional protection and become more fearful, angry, or self-protective.
content.injection: Post-war civilians must not behave like normal background crowds. They are frightened, distrustful, and politically volatile.

entries.title: Deku Hospital State Is Physically And Emotionally Transitional

context.label: Surviving students and heroes wake to losses

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Deku wakes after catastrophic overuse, new OFA knowledge, and a collapsing society that pushes him toward leaving U.A.
content.injection: Hospital Deku should feel transitional: injured, burdened, and moving toward isolation rather than restored normal school life.

entries.title: One For All Secret Narrows Around A Few Trusted People

context.label: Deku confronts One For All vestiges in hospital state

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After the war, One For All’s truth becomes more urgent but still remains restricted to a small circle.
content.injection: Do not make OFA public after the war. Its secrecy is strained, not erased.

entries.title: Vestiges Debate Whether Shigaraki Can Be Saved

context.label: Deku confronts One For All vestiges in hospital state

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The OFA vestige conversation reframes Shigaraki as someone Deku may want to save, not merely an enemy to kill.
content.injection: In OFA council scenes, let the saving-killing question matter. Deku’s later choices grow from this burden.

entries.title: Deku Leaves U.A. To Reduce Danger To Others

context.label: Deku leaves U.A. to protect others

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Deku leaves U.A. because One For All makes him a target and he believes staying near classmates endangers them.
content.injection: At the end of this deck, Deku’s departure should be protective, isolating, and fear-driven, setting up Dark Hero rather than resolving it.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Class 1-A Does Not Yet Fully Understand Deku’s Departure

context.label: Deku leaves U.A. to protect others

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: When Deku leaves U.A., his classmates receive limited explanation and do not yet fully share the OFA burden.
content.injection: Do not have Class 1-A immediately understand every OFA secret or accept Deku’s isolation as resolved.

entries.title: All Might Can Support Deku But Cannot Carry The Fight Physically

context.label: Deku leaves U.A. to protect others

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: After the war, All Might remains emotionally and strategically important to Deku but cannot fight as the Symbol of Peace.
content.injection: Use All Might as mentor support, not battlefield solution. His post-retirement limits still matter.
